title: Current Electricity
subject: Physics
content:
# Current Electricity

## Quick Synopsis

Current electricity converts circuits into equations using Ohm’s law, Kirchhoff laws, and network reduction. Measurement devices and bridges are common JEE scoring areas.

## Must Know

- Current is rate of flow of charge; conventional current direction is opposite electron drift.
- Resistance depends on material, length, area, and temperature.
- Kirchhoff junction law comes from charge conservation.
- Kirchhoff loop law comes from energy conservation.
- Ideal ammeter has zero resistance; ideal voltmeter has infinite resistance.
- Balanced Wheatstone bridge has no current through galvanometer branch.

## Formulas / Rules

- I = nqAvd.
- Ohm law: V = IR.
- Resistance: R = ρL/A.
- Series: Req = R1 + R2 + ...; parallel: 1/Req = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + ...
- Power: P = VI = I²R = V²/R.
- Cell terminal voltage while discharging: V = E - Ir.
- Wheatstone balance: P/Q = R/S.

## JEE Focus

- Reduce symmetry circuits by identifying equal-potential points.
- Use loop currents when direct reduction fails.
- For meter bridge and potentiometer, understand balance condition rather than memorizing only formula.
- Check whether source internal resistance is included.

## Common Traps

- Potential difference across ideal wire is zero.
- A voltmeter changes circuit if resistance is not very high.
- Equivalent resistance depends on terminal pair chosen.
- Power rating questions require checking both voltage and resistance constraints.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you solve multi-loop circuits?
- Can you identify balanced bridge?
- Can you include internal resistance?
- Can you convert galvanometer to ammeter/voltmeter?
